248: func (d *validatingDispatcher) callHook(ctx context.Context, h *v1.ValidatingWebhook, invocation *generic.WebhookInvocation, attr *admission.VersionedAttributes) error {
249: 	if attr.Attributes.IsDryRun() {
250: 		if h.SideEffects == nil {
251: 			return &webhookutil.ErrCallingWebhook{WebhookName: h.Name, Reason: fmt.Errorf("Webhook SideEffects is nil"), Status: apierrors.NewBadRequest("Webhook SideEffects is nil")}
252: 		}
253: 		if !(*h.SideEffects == v1.SideEffectClassNone || *h.SideEffects == v1.SideEffectClassNoneOnDryRun) {
254: 			return webhookerrors.NewDryRunUnsupportedErr(h.Name)
255: 		}
256: 	}
257: 
258: 	uid, request, response, err := webhookrequest.CreateAdmissionObjects(attr, invocation)
259: 	if err != nil {
260: 		return &webhookutil.ErrCallingWebhook{WebhookName: h.Name, Reason: fmt.Errorf("could not create admission objects: %w", err), Status: apierrors.NewBadRequest("error creating admission objects")}
261: 	}
262: 	// Make the webhook request
263: 	client, err := invocation.Webhook.GetRESTClient(d.cm)
264: 	if err != nil {
265: 		return &webhookutil.ErrCallingWebhook{WebhookName: h.Name, Reason: fmt.Errorf("could not get REST client: %w", err), Status: apierrors.NewBadRequest("error getting REST client")}
266: 	}
267: 	ctx, span := tracing.Start(ctx, "Call validating webhook",
268: 		attribute.String("configuration", invocation.Webhook.GetConfigurationName()),
269: 		attribute.String("webhook", h.Name),
270: 		attribute.Stringer("resource", attr.GetResource()),
271: 		attribute.String("subresource", attr.GetSubresource()),
272: 		attribute.String("operation", string(attr.GetOperation())),
273: 		attribute.String("UID", string(uid)))
274: 	defer span.End(500 * time.Millisecond)
275: 
276: 	// if the webhook has a specific timeout, wrap the context to apply it
277: 	if h.TimeoutSeconds != nil {
278: 		var cancel context.CancelFunc
279: 		ctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Duration(*h.TimeoutSeconds)*time.Second)
280: 		defer cancel()
281: 	}
282: 
283: 	r := client.Post().Body(request)
284: 
285: 	// if the context has a deadline, set it as a parameter to inform the backend
286: 	if deadline, hasDeadline := ctx.Deadline(); hasDeadline {
287: 		// compute the timeout
288: 		if timeout := time.Until(deadline); timeout > 0 {
289: 			// if it's not an even number of seconds, round up to the nearest second
290: 			if truncated := timeout.Truncate(time.Second); truncated != timeout {
291: 				timeout = truncated + time.Second
292: 			}
293: 			// set the timeout
294: 			r.Timeout(timeout)
295: 		}
296: 	}
297: 
298: 	do := func() { err = r.Do(ctx).Into(response) }
299: 	if wd, ok := endpointsrequest.LatencyTrackersFrom(ctx); ok {
300: 		tmp := do
301: 		do = func() { wd.ValidatingWebhookTracker.Track(tmp) }
302: 	}
303: 	do()
304: 	if err != nil {
305: 		var status *apierrors.StatusError
306: 		if se, ok := err.(*apierrors.StatusError); ok {
307: 			status = se
308: 		} else {
309: 			status = apierrors.NewBadRequest("error calling webhook")
310: 		}
311: 		return &webhookutil.ErrCallingWebhook{WebhookName: h.Name, Reason: fmt.Errorf("failed to call webhook: %w", err), Status: status}
312: 	}
313: 	span.AddEvent("Request completed")
314: 
315: 	result, err := webhookrequest.VerifyAdmissionResponse(uid, false, response)
316: 	if err != nil {
317: 		return &webhookutil.ErrCallingWebhook{WebhookName: h.Name, Reason: fmt.Errorf("received invalid webhook response: %w", err), Status: apierrors.NewServiceUnavailable("error validating webhook response")}
318: 	}
319: 
320: 	for k, v := range result.AuditAnnotations {
321: 		key := h.Name + "/" + k
322: 		if err := attr.Attributes.AddAnnotation(key, v); err != nil {
323: 			klog.Warningf("Failed to set admission audit annotation %s to %s for validating webhook %s: %v", key, v, h.Name, err)
324: 		}
325: 	}
326: 	for _, w := range result.Warnings {
327: 		warning.AddWarning(ctx, "", w)
328: 	}
329: 	if result.Allowed {
330: 		return nil
331: 	}
332: 	return &webhookutil.ErrWebhookRejection{Status: webhookerrors.ToStatusErr(h.Name, result.Result)}
333: }

124: 
125: 	for _, c := range configurations {
126: 		cachedConfigurationAccessors, ok := v.configurationsCache.Load(c.Name)
127: 		if ok {
128: 			// Pick an already cached webhookAccessor
129: 			accessors = append(accessors, cachedConfigurationAccessors.([]webhook.WebhookAccessor)...)
130: 			continue
131: 		}
132: 
133: 		// webhook names are not validated for uniqueness, so we check for duplicates and
134: 		// add a int suffix to distinguish between them
135: 		names := map[string]int{}
136: 		configurationAccessors := make([]webhook.WebhookAccessor, 0, len(c.Webhooks))
137: 		for i := range c.Webhooks {
138: 			n := c.Webhooks[i].Name
139: 			uid := fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s/%d", c.Name, n, names[n])
140: 			names[n]++
141: 			configurationAccessor := v.createValidatingWebhookAccessor(uid, c.Name, &c.Webhooks[i])
142: 			configurationAccessors = append(configurationAccessors, configurationAccessor)
143: 		}
144: 		accessors = append(accessors, configurationAccessors...)
145: 		v.configurationsCache.Store(c.Name, configurationAccessors)
146: 	}
147: 
148: 	return accessors
149: }
150: 
151: type ValidatingWebhookConfigurationSorter []*v1.ValidatingWebhookConfiguration
152: 
153: func (a ValidatingWebhookConfigurationSorter) ByName(i, j int) bool {
154: 	return a[i].Name < a[j].Name
155: }

28: const (
29: 	defaultInterval             = 1 * time.Second
30: 	defaultFailureThreshold     = 5
31: 	defaultBootstrapRetries     = 5
32: 	defaultBootstrapGraceperiod = 5 * time.Second
33: )

36: 	ErrNotReady = fmt.Errorf("configuration is not ready")
37: 	ErrDisabled = fmt.Errorf("disabled")

40: type getFunc func() (runtime.Object, error)
41: 
42: // When running, poller calls `get` every `interval`. If `get` is
43: // successful, `Ready()` returns ready and `configuration()` returns the
44: // `mergedConfiguration`; if `get` has failed more than `failureThreshold ` times,
45: // `Ready()` returns not ready and `configuration()` returns nil configuration.

47: // doing consistent read.

49: 	// a function to consistently read the latest configuration
50: 	get getFunc
51: 	// consistent read interval
52: 	// read-only
53: 	interval time.Duration
54: 	// if the number of consecutive read failure equals or exceeds the failureThreshold , the
55: 	// configuration is regarded as not ready.
56: 	// read-only
57: 	failureThreshold int
58: 	// number of consecutive failures so far.
59: 	failures int
60: 	// If the poller has passed the bootstrap phase. The poller is considered
61: 	// bootstrapped either bootstrapGracePeriod after the first call of
62: 	// configuration(), or when setConfigurationAndReady() is called, whichever
63: 	// comes first.
64: 	bootstrapped bool
65: 	// configuration() retries bootstrapRetries times if poller is not bootstrapped
66: 	// read-only
67: 	bootstrapRetries int
68: 	// Grace period for bootstrapping
69: 	// read-only
70: 	bootstrapGracePeriod time.Duration
71: 	once                 sync.Once
72: 	// if the configuration is regarded as ready.
73: 	ready               bool
74: 	mergedConfiguration runtime.Object
75: 	lastErr             error

77: 	lock sync.RWMutex
78: }

80: func newPoller(get getFunc) *poller {

82: 		get:                  get,
83: 		interval:             defaultInterval,
84: 		failureThreshold:     defaultFailureThreshold,
85: 		bootstrapRetries:     defaultBootstrapRetries,
86: 		bootstrapGracePeriod: defaultBootstrapGraceperiod,
87: 	}
88: 	return &p
89: }
90: 
91: func (a *poller) lastError(err error) {
92: 	a.lock.Lock()
93: 	defer a.lock.Unlock()
94: 	a.lastErr = err
95: }
96: 
97: func (a *poller) notReady() {
98: 	a.lock.Lock()
99: 	defer a.lock.Unlock()
100: 	a.ready = false
101: }
102: 
103: func (a *poller) bootstrapping() {
104: 	// bootstrapGracePeriod is read-only, so no lock is required
105: 	timer := time.NewTimer(a.bootstrapGracePeriod)
106: 	go func() {
107: 		defer timer.Stop()
108: 		<-timer.C
109: 		a.lock.Lock()
110: 		defer a.lock.Unlock()
111: 		a.bootstrapped = true

115: // If the poller is not bootstrapped yet, the configuration() gets a few chances
116: // to retry. This hides transient failures during system startup.
117: func (a *poller) configuration() (runtime.Object, error) {
118: 	a.once.Do(a.bootstrapping)
119: 	a.lock.RLock()
120: 	defer a.lock.RUnlock()
121: 	retries := 1
122: 	if !a.bootstrapped {
123: 		retries = a.bootstrapRetries
124: 	}
125: 	for count := 0; count < retries; count++ {
126: 		if count > 0 {
127: 			a.lock.RUnlock()
128: 			time.Sleep(a.interval)
129: 			a.lock.RLock()
130: 		}
131: 		if a.ready {
132: 			return a.mergedConfiguration, nil
133: 		}
134: 	}
135: 	if a.lastErr != nil {
136: 		return nil, a.lastErr
137: 	}
138: 	return nil, ErrNotReady
139: }
140: 
141: func (a *poller) setConfigurationAndReady(value runtime.Object) {
142: 	a.lock.Lock()
143: 	defer a.lock.Unlock()
144: 	a.bootstrapped = true
145: 	a.mergedConfiguration = value
146: 	a.ready = true
147: 	a.lastErr = nil
148: }

150: func (a *poller) Run(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {
151: 	go wait.Until(a.sync, a.interval, stopCh)
152: }
153: 
154: func (a *poller) sync() {
155: 	configuration, err := a.get()
156: 	if err != nil {
157: 		a.failures++
158: 		a.lastError(err)
159: 		if a.failures >= a.failureThreshold {
160: 			a.notReady()
161: 		}
162: 		return
163: 	}
164: 	a.failures = 0
165: 	a.setConfigurationAndReady(configuration)

43: // VerifyAdmissionResponse checks the validity of the provided admission review object, and returns the
44: // audit annotations, whether the response allowed the request, any provided patch/patchType/status,

62: 		}

64: 		patch := []byte(nil)
65: 		patchType := admissionv1.PatchType("")
66: 
67: 		if mutating {
68: 			// Ensure a mutating webhook provides both patch and patchType together
69: 			if len(r.Response.Patch) > 0 && r.Response.PatchType == nil {
70: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("webhook returned response.patch but not response.patchType")
71: 			}
72: 			if len(r.Response.Patch) == 0 && r.Response.PatchType != nil {
73: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("webhook returned response.patchType but not response.patch")
74: 			}
75: 			patch = r.Response.Patch
76: 			if r.Response.PatchType != nil {
77: 				patchType = *r.Response.PatchType
78: 				if len(patchType) == 0 {
79: 					return nil, fmt.Errorf("webhook returned invalid response.patchType of %q", patchType)
80: 				}
81: 			}
82: 		} else {
83: 			// Ensure a validating webhook doesn't return patch or patchType
84: 			if len(r.Response.Patch) > 0 {
85: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("validating webhook may not return response.patch")
86: 			}
87: 			if r.Response.PatchType != nil {
88: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("validating webhook may not return response.patchType")
89: 			}
90: 		}

92: 		return &AdmissionResponse{
93: 			AuditAnnotations: r.Response.AuditAnnotations,
94: 			Allowed:          r.Response.Allowed,
95: 			Patch:            patch,
96: 			PatchType:        patchType,
97: 			Result:           r.Response.Result,
98: 			Warnings:         r.Response.Warnings,
99: 		}, nil

101: 	case *admissionv1beta1.AdmissionReview:

106: 		// Response GVK and response.uid were not verified in v1beta1 handling, allow any

114: 				patchType = admissionv1.PatchTypeJSONPatch

125: 		}, nil

134: func CreateAdmissionObjects(versionedAttributes *admission.VersionedAttributes, invocation *generic.WebhookInvocation) (uid types.UID, request, response runtime.Object, err error) {
135: 	for _, version := range invocation.Webhook.GetAdmissionReviewVersions() {

25: const (
26: 	// timeToWaitForReady is the amount of time to wait to let an admission controller to be ready to satisfy a request.
27: 	// this is useful when admission controllers need to warm their caches before letting requests through.
28: 	timeToWaitForReady = 10 * time.Second
29: )

31: // ReadyFunc is a function that returns true if the admission controller is ready to handle requests.
32: type ReadyFunc func() bool
33: 
34: // Handler is a base for admission control handlers that
35: // support a predefined set of operations
36: type Handler struct {
37: 	operations sets.String
38: 	readyFunc  ReadyFunc
39: }
40: 
41: // Handles returns true for methods that this handler supports
42: func (h *Handler) Handles(operation Operation) bool {
43: 	return h.operations.Has(string(operation))
44: }
45: 
46: // NewHandler creates a new base handler that handles the passed
47: // in operations
48: func NewHandler(ops ...Operation) *Handler {
49: 	operations := sets.NewString()
50: 	for _, op := range ops {
51: 		operations.Insert(string(op))
52: 	}
53: 	return &Handler{
54: 		operations: operations,
55: 	}
56: }
57: 
58: // SetReadyFunc allows late registration of a ReadyFunc to know if the handler is ready to process requests.
59: func (h *Handler) SetReadyFunc(readyFunc ReadyFunc) {
60: 	h.readyFunc = readyFunc
61: }
62: 
63: // WaitForReady will wait for the readyFunc (if registered) to return ready, and in case of timeout, will return false.
64: func (h *Handler) WaitForReady() bool {
65: 	// there is no ready func configured, so we return immediately
66: 	if h.readyFunc == nil {
67: 		return true
68: 	}
69: 
70: 	timeout := time.After(timeToWaitForReady)
71: 	for !h.readyFunc() {
72: 		select {
73: 		case <-time.After(100 * time.Millisecond):
74: 		case <-timeout:
75: 			return h.readyFunc()
76: 		}
77: 	}
78: 	return true
79: }

58: // AdmissionOptions holds the admission options
59: type AdmissionOptions struct {
60: 	// RecommendedPluginOrder holds an ordered list of plugin names we recommend to use by default
61: 	RecommendedPluginOrder []string
62: 	// DefaultOffPlugins is a set of plugin names that is disabled by default
63: 	DefaultOffPlugins sets.Set[string]
64: 
65: 	// EnablePlugins indicates plugins to be enabled passed through `--enable-admission-plugins`.
66: 	EnablePlugins []string
67: 	// DisablePlugins indicates plugins to be disabled passed through `--disable-admission-plugins`.
68: 	DisablePlugins []string
69: 	// ConfigFile is the file path with admission control configuration.
70: 	ConfigFile string
71: 	// Plugins contains all registered plugins.
72: 	Plugins *admission.Plugins
73: 	// Decorators is a list of admission decorator to wrap around the admission plugins
74: 	Decorators admission.Decorators
75: }
76: 
77: // NewAdmissionOptions creates a new instance of AdmissionOptions
78: // Note:
79: //
80: //	In addition it calls RegisterAllAdmissionPlugins to register
81: //	all generic admission plugins.
82: //
83: //	Provides the list of RecommendedPluginOrder that holds sane values
84: //	that can be used by servers that don't care about admission chain.
85: //	Servers that do care can overwrite/append that field after creation.
86: func NewAdmissionOptions() *AdmissionOptions {
87: 	options := &AdmissionOptions{
88: 		Plugins:    admission.NewPlugins(),
89: 		Decorators: admission.Decorators{admission.DecoratorFunc(admissionmetrics.WithControllerMetrics)},
90: 		// This list is mix of mutating admission plugins and validating
91: 		// admission plugins. The apiserver always runs the validating ones
92: 		// after all the mutating ones, so their relative order in this list
93: 		// doesn't matter.
94: 		RecommendedPluginOrder: []string{lifecycle.PluginName, mutatingadmissionpolicy.PluginName, mutatingwebhook.PluginName, validatingadmissionpolicy.PluginName, validatingwebhook.PluginName},
95: 		DefaultOffPlugins:      sets.Set[string]{},
96: 	}
97: 	server.RegisterAllAdmissionPlugins(options.Plugins)
98: 	return options
99: }

steps.message:
Here is the complete, authoritative map of every Go source file in Kubernetes v1.32.0 that implements the four aspects of the API server admission controller pipeline.

---

## 1. `admission.Interface` Registration

These files define the core plugin interface, the plugin registry, and the two registration call-sites.

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go` | Defines `Interface`, `MutationInterface`, `ValidationInterface`, `PluginInitializer`, `Attributes`, `ObjectInterfaces`, `InitializationValidator` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugins.go` | `Plugins` registry struct; `Register()`, `NewFromPlugins()`, `InitPlugin()` — the factory that instantiates and initializes every plugin |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/handler.go` | `Handler` base struct embedded by every plugin; `NewHandler()`, `Handles()`, `WaitForReady()` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/decorator.go` | `Decorator` / `DecoratorFunc` / `Decorators` — wraps plugins (e.g. with metrics) at construction time |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/plugins.go` | Generic `RegisterAllAdmissionPlugins()` — registers `NamespaceLifecycle`, `MutatingAdmissionPolicy`, `MutatingAdmissionWebhook`, `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy`, `ValidatingAdmissionWebhook` |
| `pkg/kubeapiserver/options/plugins.go` | Kube-specific `RegisterAllAdmissionPlugins()` and `AllOrderedPlugins` — the canonical ordered list of ~30 in-tree plugins (AlwaysAdmit → NodeRestriction → …) |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/options/admission.go` | `AdmissionOptions` / `NewAdmissionOptions()` / `ApplyTo()` — reads config, calls `Plugins.NewFromPlugins()`, wraps result with step metrics, and assigns to `c.AdmissionControl` |

**Key mechanics:**
- `plugins.Register(name, factory)` stores a `Factory func(io.Reader) (Interface, error)` in a map.
- `NewFromPlugins(names, configProvider, initializers, decorators)` iterates the ordered name list, calls each factory, runs every `PluginInitializer.Initialize(plugin)`, applies `Decorators`, and feeds the result into `newReinvocationHandler(chain)`.
- `ApplyTo` sets `c.AdmissionControl = admissionmetrics.WithStepMetrics(admissionChain)`.

---

## 2. Generic Admission Chain Execution

These files build and execute the ordered chain of all enabled plugins.

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/chain.go` | `chainAdmissionHandler` — iterates plugins; `Admit()` calls only `MutationInterface` implementors, `Validate()` calls only `ValidationInterface` implementors; the chain always runs all mutating plugins before any validating ones |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/reinvocation.go` | `reinvoker` / `newReinvocationHandler()` — wraps the chain; after the first pass, if any mutating plugin set `SetShouldReinvoke()`, re-runs the mutating phase (up to `reinvocationLimit = 2` rounds) |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/metrics/metrics.go` | `WithControllerMetrics` (per-plugin decorator), `WithStepMetrics` (whole-chain decorator), `WithMetrics` — emit `apiserver_admission_controller_admission_duration_seconds` and `apiserver_admission_step_admission_duration_seconds` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/initializer/initializer.go` | Generic `pluginInitializer` — injects `kubernetes.Interface`, `dynamic.Interface`, `SharedInformerFactory`, `authorizer.Authorizer`, `featuregate.FeatureGate`, `RESTMapper` into any plugin that implements the corresponding `Wants*` interface |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/initializer/interfaces.go` | Declares all `Wants*` interfaces: `WantsExternalKubeClientSet`, `WantsExternalKubeInformerFactory`, `WantsDynamicClient`, `WantsAuthorizer`, `WantsFeatures`, `WantsDrainedNotification`, `WantsRESTMapper` |

**Key mechanics in `chain.go`:**
```go
// Admit — mutating pass only
for _, handler := range chain {
    if mutator, ok := handler.(MutationInterface); ok {
        mutator.Admit(ctx, a, o)
    }
}
// Validate — validating pass only
for _, handler := range chain {
    if validator, ok := handler.(ValidationInterface); ok {
        validator.Validate(ctx, a, o)
    }
}
```
`reinvocation.go` wraps this so that if `attr.GetReinvocationContext().ShouldReinvoke()` is true after the first mutating pass, the mutating pass is repeated.

---

## 3. Mutating Admission Ordering

These files implement the `MutatingAdmissionWebhook` plugin, its sequential ordering, and the reinvocation protocol.

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/plugin.go` | `Plugin` struct (`var _ admission.MutationInterface = &Plugin{}`); `Register()` calls `plugins.Register("MutatingAdmissionWebhook", …)`; `Admit()` delegates to `Webhook.Dispatch()` |

| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/mutating/reinvocationcontext.go` | `webhookReinvokeContext` — tracks `previouslyInvokedReinvocableWebhooks` and `reinvokeWebhooks` sets; `RequireReinvokingPreviouslyInvokedPlugins()` promotes the "previously invoked" set to the "must reinvoke" set when a later mutation occurs |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/generic/webhook.go` | `Webhook` struct (embedded by both mutating and validating `Plugin`); `NewWebhook()` wires `Source` + `Dispatcher`; `Dispatch()` calls `source.Webhooks()` then `dispatcher.Dispatch(ctx, attr, o, hooks)`; `ShouldCallHook()` evaluates namespace/object selectors and CEL match conditions |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/generic/interfaces.go` | `Dispatcher` interface (`Dispatch(ctx, attr, o, hooks)`), `Source` interface (`Webhooks() []WebhookAccessor`, `HasSynced()`), `WebhookInvocation`, `VersionedAttributeAccessor` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/accessors.go` | `WebhookAccessor` interface; `mutatingWebhookAccessor` / `validatingWebhookAccessor` concrete types; `NewMutatingWebhookAccessor()` / `NewValidatingWebhookAccessor()` constructors; lazy-compiled CEL match conditions and object/namespace selectors |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/configuration/mutating_webhook_manager.go` | `mutatingWebhookConfigurationManager` — watches `MutatingWebhookConfiguration` objects via informer; `Webhooks()` returns a **name-sorted**, lazily-evaluated `[]WebhookAccessor`; caches accessors per configuration to avoid CEL recompilation |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/configuration/configuration_manager.go` | `poller` — generic polling mechanism (1 s interval, 5-failure threshold) used by both webhook managers for bootstrap readiness |

**Ordering guarantee:** `mutatingWebhookConfigurationManager.getValidatingWebhookConfigurations` (sic — same pattern in mutating) calls `sort.SliceStable(configurations, ByName)`, so configurations are processed alphabetically by name; within a configuration, webhooks are in declaration order.

**Reinvocation protocol (dispatcher.go lines 106–122, 192–199):**
1. On first call, `webhookReinvokeCtx` is created fresh.
2. On reinvoke call, if the object changed since the last webhook invocation (`IsOutputChangedSinceLastWebhookInvocation`), `RequireReinvokingPreviouslyInvokedPlugins()` is called.
3. Webhooks with `ReinvocationPolicy: IfNeeded` are added to `previouslyInvokedReinvocableWebhooks`.
4. If a patch changes the object, `reinvokeCtx.SetShouldReinvoke()` triggers the outer `reinvocation.go` loop.

---

## 4. Validating Admission Webhook Call

These files implement the `ValidatingAdmissionWebhook` plugin and its parallel HTTP dispatch.

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/validating/plugin.go` | `Plugin` struct (`var _ admission.ValidationInterface = &Plugin{}`); `Register()` calls `plugins.Register("ValidatingAdmissionWebhook", …)`; `Validate()` delegates to `Webhook.Dispatch()` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/validating/dispatcher.go` | `validatingDispatcher.Dispatch()` — first filters to `relevantHooks` (calling `ShouldCallHook`), then fans out **in parallel** via `sync.WaitGroup` + goroutines; each goroutine calls `callHook()` which POSTs the `AdmissionReview` and returns `ErrWebhookRejection` or `ErrCallingWebhook`; errors are collected in a buffered channel and the first error is returned |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/configuration/validating_webhook_manager.go` | `validatingWebhookConfigurationManager` — watches `ValidatingWebhookConfiguration` objects; `Webhooks()` returns name-sorted `[]WebhookAccessor`; same lazy + cache pattern as the mutating manager |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/request/admissionreview.go` | `CreateAdmissionObjects()` — selects `v1` or `v1beta1` `AdmissionReview` based on `GetAdmissionReviewVersions()`; `CreateV1AdmissionReview()` / `CreateV1beta1AdmissionReview()` — marshal `VersionedAttributes` into the wire format; `VerifyAdmissionResponse()` — validates UID match, GVK, and enforces that validating webhooks cannot return `patch`/`patchType` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/plugin/webhook/matchconditions/matcher.go` | `matcher` — evaluates compiled CEL expressions (`MatchCondition`) against the request; called by `ShouldCallHook()` in `generic/webhook.go`; respects `FailurePolicy` (Fail/Ignore) on CEL evaluation errors |

**Key difference from mutating:** validating webhooks run **in parallel** (goroutines) and cannot mutate the object — `VerifyAdmissionResponse(uid, false, response)` explicitly rejects any response containing `patch` or `patchType`.

| `pkg/kubeapiserver/admission/initializer.go` | Kube-specific `PluginInitializer` — injects `cloudConfig []byte` into plugins implementing `WantsCloudConfig` |
| `pkg/kubeapiserver/admission/config.go` | `Config.New()` — reads cloud config file and returns `[]admission.PluginInitializer{NewPluginInitializer(cloudConfig)}` |

## End-to-End Data Flow Summary

```
kube-apiserver startup
  └─ AdmissionOptions.ApplyTo()                          [server/options/admission.go]
       ├─ Plugins.NewFromPlugins(orderedNames, …)         [admission/plugins.go]
       │    ├─ factory() → Plugin                         [each plugin's plugin.go]
       │    ├─ PluginInitializer.Initialize(plugin)       [initializer/initializer.go,
       │    │                                              webhook/initializer/initializer.go,
       │    │                                              kubeapiserver/admission/initializer.go]
       │    └─ Decorators.Decorate(plugin, name)          [admission/decorator.go]
       │         └─ WithControllerMetrics(plugin, name)   [admission/metrics/metrics.go]
       ├─ newReinvocationHandler(chainAdmissionHandler)   [admission/reinvocation.go,
       │                                                   admission/chain.go]
       └─ c.AdmissionControl = WithStepMetrics(chain)     [admission/metrics/metrics.go]

Per-request (e.g. CREATE Pod)
  └─ reinvoker.Admit(ctx, attr, o)                       [admission/reinvocation.go]
       └─ chainAdmissionHandler.Admit()                   [admission/chain.go]
            └─ MutatingAdmissionWebhook.Admit()           [webhook/mutating/plugin.go]
                 └─ generic.Webhook.Dispatch()            [webhook/generic/webhook.go]
                      ├─ source.Webhooks()                [configuration/mutating_webhook_manager.go]
                      └─ mutatingDispatcher.Dispatch()    [webhook/mutating/dispatcher.go]
                           ├─ ShouldCallHook()            [generic/webhook.go]
                           │    └─ matcher.Match()        [webhook/matchconditions/matcher.go]
                           └─ callAttrMutatingHook()
                                ├─ CreateAdmissionObjects [webhook/request/admissionreview.go]
                                ├─ client.Post().Do()
                                └─ VerifyAdmissionResponse + apply JSON patch

  └─ chainAdmissionHandler.Validate()                    [admission/chain.go]
       └─ ValidatingAdmissionWebhook.Validate()          [webhook/validating/plugin.go]
            └─ generic.Webhook.Dispatch()                [webhook/generic/webhook.go]
                 ├─ source.Webhooks()                    [configuration/validating_webhook_manager.go]
                 └─ validatingDispatcher.Dispatch()      [webhook/validating/dispatcher.go]
                      └─ parallel goroutines → callHook()
                           ├─ CreateAdmissionObjects     [webhook/request/admissionreview.go]
                           ├─ client.Post().Do()
                           └─ VerifyAdmissionResponse (mutating=false)
```

          "key_symbols": [
            "PluginInitializer",
            "NewPluginInitializer",
            "Initialize",
            "WantsCloudConfig"
          ],
          "role": "Kube-specific PluginInitializer: injects cloudConfig []byte into plugins implementing WantsCloudConfig."
        },
        {
          "path": "pkg/kubeapiserver/admission/config.go",
          "package": "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubeapiserver/admission",
          "key_symbols": [
            "Config",
            "New"
          ],
          "role": "Config.New() reads the cloud config file and returns []admission.PluginInitializer{NewPluginInitializer(cloudConfig)}."
        }
      ]
    }
  },
  "end_to_end_flow": {
    "startup": [
      "AdmissionOptions.ApplyTo() [server/options/admission.go]",
      "  -> Plugins.NewFromPlugins(orderedNames, configProvider, initializers, decorators) [admission/plugins.go]",
      "       -> factory() -> Plugin  [each plugin's plugin.go]",
      "       -> PluginInitializer.Initialize(plugin)  [initializer/initializer.go, webhook/initializer/initializer.go, kubeapiserver/admission/initializer.go]",
      "       -> Decorators.Decorate(plugin, name) -> WithControllerMetrics  [admission/decorator.go, admission/metrics/metrics.go]",
      "  -> newReinvocationHandler(chainAdmissionHandler)  [admission/reinvocation.go, admission/chain.go]",
      "  -> c.AdmissionControl = WithStepMetrics(chain)  [admission/metrics/metrics.go]"
    ],
    "per_request": [
      "reinvoker.Admit(ctx, attr, o)  [admission/reinvocation.go]",
      "  -> chainAdmissionHandler.Admit()  [admission/chain.go]",
      "       -> MutatingAdmissionWebhook.Admit()  [webhook/mutating/plugin.go]",
      "            -> generic.Webhook.Dispatch()  [webhook/generic/webhook.go]",
      "                 -> source.Webhooks()  [configuration/mutating_webhook_manager.go]",
      "                 -> mutatingDispatcher.Dispatch() [webhook/mutating/dispatcher.go]",
      "                      -> ShouldCallHook() -> matcher.Match()  [generic/webhook.go, webhook/matchconditions/matcher.go]",
      "                      -> callAttrMutatingHook()",
      "                           -> CreateAdmissionObjects()  [webhook/request/admissionreview.go]",
      "                           -> client.Post().Do()",
      "                           -> VerifyAdmissionResponse() + apply JSON patch",
      "                           -> reinvokeCtx.SetShouldReinvoke() if object changed",
      "  [reinvoker re-runs mutating pass if ShouldReinvoke() == true]",
      "chainAdmissionHandler.Validate()  [admission/chain.go]",
      "  -> ValidatingAdmissionWebhook.Validate()  [webhook/validating/plugin.go]",
      "       -> generic.Webhook.Dispatch()  [webhook/generic/webhook.go]",
      "            -> source.Webhooks()  [configuration/validating_webhook_manager.go]",
      "            -> validatingDispatcher.Dispatch()  [webhook/validating/dispatcher.go]",
      "                 -> parallel goroutines -> callHook()",
      "                      -> CreateAdmissionObjects()  [webhook/request/admissionreview.go]",
      "                      -> client.Post().Do()",
      "                      -> VerifyAdmissionResponse(mutating=false)"
    ]
  },
  "key_design_decisions": {
    "mutating_vs_validating_ordering": "chainAdmissionHandler.Admit() runs ALL MutationInterface plugins before chainAdmissionHandler.Validate() runs ANY ValidationInterface plugin, regardless of their position in AllOrderedPlugins.",
    "mutating_webhook_ordering": "Webhooks within MutatingAdmissionWebhook are processed SEQUENTIALLY in name-sorted configuration order, then declaration order within each configuration.",
    "validating_webhook_parallelism": "Webhooks within ValidatingAdmissionWebhook are dispatched IN PARALLEL via goroutines; the first error is returned.",
    "reinvocation": "MutatingAdmissionWebhook supports IfNeeded reinvocation: if a later webhook (or in-tree plugin) mutates the object, previously-invoked webhooks with ReinvocationPolicy=IfNeeded are re-run. The outer reinvoker in reinvocation.go handles re-running the entire mutating chain.",
    "patch_enforcement": "VerifyAdmissionResponse(uid, mutating=false, response) explicitly rejects any validating webhook response that contains patch or patchType fields.",
    "configuration_sorting": "Both mutating and validating webhook configuration managers sort configurations alphabetically by name (sort.SliceStable + ByName) to ensure deterministic ordering."
  }
}
